regime.label: Hawkish Melt-Up
regime.sublabel: Record S&P/TSX Highs vs. Warsh Fed Pivot + Hot Inflation + Fear & Greed at 27
regime.description: The S&P 500 closed at a record 7,440 (+9% YTD) on June 29 and the TSX cleared 35,600 intraday — but the rally is narrow and unconfirmed by sentiment: the Nasdaq actually fell ~3.4% in June and the CNN Fear & Greed Index sits at 27 (Fear), a rare divergence from a record tape. The driver is almost entirely an AI capex supercycle (MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, META, NVDA collectively committing $600B+ for 2026) running against a genuinely hostile macro backdrop: new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first FOMC (June 17) flipped the dot plot hawkish — median 2026 year-end rate now higher, not lower, with markets pricing ~62% odds of a September hike — while May CPI accelerated to 4.2% YoY on an Iran-war energy shock. A fragile 60-day US-Iran ceasefire MOU (June 18) already cracked once on a June 22 strike threat.

riskMeter.label: Melt-Up, Not Confirmation
riskMeter.sublabel: AI capex is real and self-funding, but a hawkish Fed pivot, 4.2% CPI, and Fear & Greed at 27 mean this rally has far less macro support than the index level suggests. Stay concentrated in the capex winners; don't chase the rest.

spreads.label: 3M–10Y
spreads.note: Fully un-inverted for the first time this cycle — front end anchored near the 3.50–3.75% Fed funds range while the 10Y holds an inflation/deficit premium at 4.39%.

spreads.label: 2Y–10Y
spreads.note: Positively sloped; a hawkish Fed pause plus sticky long-end inflation compensation has completed the un-inversion the market spent two years waiting for.

spreads.label: 5Y–30Y
spreads.note: Long end still demands a term premium given deficits and a 4.2% CPI print — the curve is normal but not signaling all-clear on inflation.

yieldCurve.inversionStatus: Fully dis-inverted across 3M–10Y and 2s10s — the curve normalized as the Fed paused at an elevated plateau while the long end priced in sticky inflation risk
yieldCurve.recessionProbability: ~13% (NY Fed 12-month model, est.) — a normal curve historically signals low near-term recession risk, though the hawkish hike-pricing is a fresh cross-current
yieldCurve.trajectory: A confirmed September hike would likely steepen the front end further and could re-test 2Y yields higher; a hold would leave the curve range-bound at current normal levels.
yieldCurve.canadianCurve: BoC held at 2.25% on June 10 for a fourth straight meeting — Canadian growth is weak and the BoC is explicitly looking through Iran-war-driven headline inflation rather than tightening into it. GoC curve is positively sloped; CAD has weakened to ~1.36–1.39 vs USD.

buffettIndicator.description: US total equity market cap has pushed to a fresh 2026 high alongside the S&P's record close — valuation has re-rated further even as the earnings backdrop is carried by a handful of AI capex names.
buffettIndicator.implication: At ~188%, there is essentially no valuation cushion left outside the capex winners; a hawkish repricing shock (confirmed September hike, hot June CPI) would hit richly-priced, non-AI-capex names hardest.

signals.label: S&P 500
signals.note: Record close June 29, +9% YTD — but the rally is concentrated in AI capex mega-caps, not broad-based.

signals.label: TSX Composite
signals.note: Record territory (intraday high 35,630 on June 17) on gold miners, Shopify, energy and financials strength.

signals.label: VIX
signals.note: Compressed again after a spike to 23.3 on the June 22 Iran strike threat — the tail risk is fatter than the level implies.

signals.label: Nasdaq / Tech
signals.note: Fell ~3.4% in June even as the S&P hit records — a genuine breadth warning underneath the index-level melt-up.

signals.label: Buffett Indicator
signals.note: New 2026 high — market cap re-rating has outrun GDP growth by a wide margin.

signals.label: Shiller CAPE
signals.note: Pushing further into dot-com-adjacent territory; AI productivity premium is doing all the justifying.

signals.label: S&P 500 Forward P/E
signals.note: Expanding further as price outruns estimate revisions outside the AI capex complex.

signals.label: S&P 500 Earnings Yield
signals.note: Equity risk premium over the 4.39% 10Y is thin — one of the least attractive stock/bond trade-offs since 2007.

signals.label: Fed Funds Rate
signals.note: Held June 17 in Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chair; dot plot flipped to show rates ending 2026 HIGHER (3.6–4.1%) — a hike, not a cut, is now the live risk.

signals.label: 10Y US Treasury
signals.note: Little changed on the month even as short-end hike odds rose — long end still pricing sticky inflation.

signals.label: Yield Curve (2s10s)
signals.note: Fully dis-inverted — the historically bullish un-inversion signal has now fired, though under an unusually hawkish backdrop.

signals.label: 10Y Real Yield
signals.note: Elevated real yields are the direct pressure behind gold's -14% quarterly drawdown.

signals.label: 10Y Breakeven Inflation
signals.note: Drifting higher with the May CPI acceleration — the disinflation narrative from earlier in 2026 has stalled.

signals.label: BoC Rate
signals.note: Held June 10 for a 4th straight meeting; BoC explicitly looking through war-driven headline inflation given weak domestic growth.

signals.label: US CPI (YoY)
signals.note: May print accelerated on the Iran-war energy shock (energy +3.9% MoM); June print due July 14 is the next major catalyst.

signals.label: US Core PCE (YoY)
signals.note: Tracking with core CPI's acceleration — the Fed's 2% target is moving further away, not closer.

signals.label: US Unemployment
signals.note: May print — labour market softening modestly but not breaking; still consistent with a soft landing on the growth side alone.

signals.label: ISM Manufacturing
signals.note: May reading, up from 52.7 — solidly expansionary; June data due July 1.

signals.label: ISM Services
signals.note: 23rd consecutive month of expansion; growth backdrop is genuinely fine even as inflation is not.

signals.label: M2 Growth (YoY)
signals.note: Still running ahead of productivity growth — a modest inflationary cross-current.

signals.label: Oil (WTI)
signals.note: Settled near $70.56 after the June 18 US-Iran ceasefire MOU, down sharply from the $84–88 wartime spike — but the ceasefire already cracked once on June 22.

signals.label: Gold
signals.note: Down 11% in June and 14% QTD as rising real yields overwhelm safe-haven demand from the still-live Iran risk.

signals.label: USD/CAD
signals.note: CAD weaker on a dovish-relative BoC vs. a hawkish Fed — a modest tailwind for CAD-cost, USD-revenue names.

signals.label: HY Credit Spread (CDX HY)
signals.note: Holding tight despite the hawkish repricing — credit hasn't yet caught up to the rates story; worth watching if hike odds keep rising.

signals.label: IG Credit Spread
signals.note: Investment-grade balance sheets remain healthy; no stress signal yet from the hawkish pivot.

signals.label: US HY Default Rate
signals.note: A resumed hike cycle would extend refinancing stress rather than let it ease as previously expected.

signals.label: Bank Lending Standards
signals.note: Credit conditions were bottoming under the old rate-cut scenario; the hawkish flip removes that tailwind.

signals.label: DXY (US Dollar Index)
signals.note: Hawkish Fed repricing is a fresh dollar tailwind — a reversal of the weak-dollar thesis that was priced a month ago.

signals.label: Copper (front-month)
signals.note: Near record highs (LME ~$13,800/tonne), up 22.6% YoY on AI data-center buildout and electrification demand — still the cleanest corroborating signal for the capex theme.

signals.label: China PMI
signals.note: Third straight month of expansion, supported by AI-linked high-tech exports even as domestic demand stays soft.

signals.label: Eurozone CPI
signals.note: Broadly at target; ECB has more room to ease than the Fed does right now.

signals.label: Fear & Greed
signals.note: The single most important signal this month: Fear sentiment against a record S&P print is a genuine, rare divergence.

signals.label: AAII Bull/Bear
signals.note: Consistent with the Fear & Greed reading — retail sentiment has not confirmed the index-level rally.

signals.label: Put/Call Ratio
signals.note: Hedging demand picked up alongside the June 22 VIX spike and hasn't fully unwound.

signals.label: US Consumer Confidence
signals.note: 4.2% CPI and elevated energy costs are pressuring the consumer-recovery thesis that was priced a month ago.

signals.label: Earnings Revisions
signals.note: Upward revisions remain concentrated in AI capex names (NVDA, MSFT, META) rather than broadening out.

newsSignals.headline: Fed Holds Rates at Warsh's First Meeting as Chair; Dot Plot Flips to Show Hikes, Not Cuts, by Year-End
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: The rate-cut playbook priced a month ago is invalidated — avoid adding long-duration bonds (ZAG.TO) or richly-valued rate-cut-dependent growth until the September meeting clarifies direction.

newsSignals.headline: Oil Prices Fall, Stocks Rally as US and Iran Sign Framework MOU to End War, Reopen Strait of Hormuz
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: WTI relief to ~$70 supports margins for Canadian oil sands producers (SU.TO) at their low break-evens, but the 60-day ceasefire window is short and unproven.

newsSignals.headline: Oil Prices Fluctuate as Trump Threatens Fresh Strikes on Iran, Rattling Strait of Hormuz Shipping
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: The ceasefire already cracked once — keep a modest CNQ/SU.TO/gold hedge live rather than assuming the Iran risk is fully resolved.

newsSignals.headline: May CPI Accelerates to 4.2% YoY as Iran-War Energy Shock Feeds Through; Core at 2.9%
newsSignals.portfolioImpact: Hot inflation plus a hawkish Fed is the core risk to any name priced for imminent rate relief; the June CPI print (July 14) is the next binary catalyst.

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: Iran ceasefire holds past its 60-day window; June/July CPI cool back toward 3%; September FOMC holds rather than hikes; AI capex earnings beat again
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 36,000–37,000; BoC stays on hold; CAD stabilizes 1.34–1.37; Canadian banks and energy grind higher on stable oil
scenarios.positioning: Add NVDA/MSFT/AVGO on dips; hold ENB/SU.TO as ceasefire-durability hedge; avoid adding bond duration until a hold is confirmed
scenarios.sectorRotation: Overweight AI infrastructure and Canadian financials; underweight long-duration bonds and rate-cut-dependent consumer discretionary

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: Fed holds again in September but keeps a hawkish tilt; Iran ceasefire holds but stays tense; CPI plateaus near 4%; AI capex carries the index while breadth stays narrow
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 34,500–36,000; BoC on hold; CAD range-bound 1.35–1.39; gold stabilizes but doesn't recover
scenarios.positioning: Stay concentrated in the AI capex chain; keep gold/energy as a hedge without adding; do not chase Nasdaq weakness or gold's drawdown
scenarios.sectorRotation: Barbell: AI infrastructure mega-caps + Canadian pipelines/banks; trim richly-priced consumer discretionary priced for a cut that isn't coming

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: September FOMC actually hikes; Iran ceasefire collapses and oil spikes back above $95; June/July CPI re-accelerate above 4.5%; Fear & Greed falls into Extreme Fear and the AI capex names finally correct
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 30,000–33,000; BoC forced to hold despite weak growth; CAD weakens sharply; energy spikes briefly then risk-off dominates
scenarios.positioning: Raise cash; add to gold and CNQ/SU.TO on the oil spike; trim high-beta/growth (BTCC, richly-priced non-capex tech) aggressively
scenarios.sectorRotation: Max defensives: energy, gold miners, Canadian banks; exit crypto and rate-cut-dependent consumer discretionary entirely

keyEvents.event: June CPI print — decides whether September is a hike or a hold
FOMC — first live hike-not-cut risk of this cycle under Chair Warsh

scenarios.label: Bull case
scenarios.trigger: Iran conflict fully resolves; inflation rolls back over toward 3% by Q4; Fed holds through year-end without hiking; AI capex earnings broaden participation beyond mega-caps
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 37,000+; BoC begins cutting into 2027 on continued weak growth; CAD strengthens to 1.30–1.34
scenarios.positioning: Increase equity allocation broadly, not just AI capex; rotate some gains into Canadian financials and energy
scenarios.sectorRotation: Broaden from mega-cap AI into financials, industrials, and quality cyclicals as breadth confirms

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.trigger: Fed hikes once in September then pauses; Iran ceasefire holds in fits and starts; inflation grinds sideways near 3.5–4%; AI capex remains the dominant earnings story
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 34,000–36,500; BoC on hold most of H2; CAD range-bound; gold finds a base once real yields peak
scenarios.positioning: Stay concentrated in AI capex and Canadian income names; keep a full but not oversized gold/energy hedge; avoid duration
scenarios.sectorRotation: Quality/capex barbell: mega-cap AI infrastructure + Canadian pipelines and banks; reduce speculative small-cap and crypto

scenarios.label: Bear case
scenarios.trigger: Fed hikes multiple times as inflation proves sticky above 4.5%; Iran conflict re-escalates; credit spreads widen sharply; AI capex names finally re-rate on valuation
scenarios.canadianAngle: TSX 27,000–30,000; BoC caught between weak growth and a weak currency; CAD weakens toward 1.45+
scenarios.positioning: Raise cash to 20-25%; full gold and energy weighting; exit crypto, high-beta growth, and long-duration bonds entirely
scenarios.sectorRotation: Maximum defensives: energy, gold, Canadian banks, short-duration cash instruments; avoid all rate-sensitive and rate-cut-dependent exposure

keyEvents.event: FOMC — the defining event of H2 2026 under new Chair Warsh
Q3 earnings — watch whether AI capex earnings broaden or the melt-up stays narrow
US midterm elections — fiscal policy and Fed independence narrative
Year-end rebalancing; second Fed decision of H2

bullish.label: AI capex supercycle intact and accelerating — MSFT $120B+, AMZN $200B, GOOGL $175-185B, META up to $135B, NVDA ~$500B secured through 2026
Copper at record highs (~$6.19/lb) on AI data-center and electrification demand — the cleanest corroborating signal for the capex theme
Yield curve fully un-inverted (2s10s +54 bps) — historically a strong 12-month forward equity signal, even under a hawkish backdrop
US-Iran 60-day ceasefire MOU holding (for now) — Strait of Hormuz reopening would remove the last major oil-supply risk premium
China PMI expanding for a third straight month on AI-linked high-tech exports

bearish.label: Fed dot plot flipped hawkish under new Chair Warsh — a September hike (62% priced) would be the first hike of this cycle and could shock richly-valued non-capex names
Iran ceasefire is fragile — already cracked once (June 22 strike threat); a full re-escalation sends oil back above $95-100
Fear & Greed at 27 against a record S&P print — a rare divergence that has historically preceded either a sharp correction or a violent breadth catch-up rally
May CPI at 4.2% and accelerating — the disinflation narrative from earlier in 2026 has stalled; July 14 print is the next binary risk
Gold down 14% QTD as real yields rise — the safe-haven trade is currently losing to the rates trade, a warning that markets aren't yet hedging the geopolitical risk properly
Buffett Indicator at ~188% (Extremely Overvalued) — zero valuation cushion outside the AI capex complex for any hawkish repricing shock

portfolioImplication.summary: The macro backdrop flipped materially hawkish in June even as the index hit records — a new Fed chair's first meeting produced a dot plot showing hikes, not cuts, while May CPI accelerated to 4.2% on a real Iran-war energy shock. The rally is narrow (Nasdaq actually fell in June) and unconfirmed by sentiment (Fear & Greed at 27), which is the single most important signal to watch. AI capex remains the one genuinely load-bearing bull case — it's contractually locked in and self-funding, which makes it the least rate-sensitive part of the market. Everything priced for imminent rate cuts (long-duration bonds, rate-cut-dependent consumer discretionary) needs to be reassessed; the July 14 CPI print and September 17 FOMC are the two events that will decide which way this resolves.

actions.action: Hold/add NVDA on dips — ~$500B of Blackwell/Rubin revenue is secured through 2026 regardless of Fed path; the least rate-sensitive name in the portfolio.
Do not add duration here — the June dot plot flip to hikes invalidates the rate-cut thesis this position was built on; hold existing weight, don't extend it.
Hold CNQ as an Iran-ceasefire-durability hedge — the MOU already cracked once on June 22; oil at $70 still covers the dividend comfortably.
Do not add to GOLD despite the live geopolitical risk — down 14% QTD as real yields rise; wait for real yields to roll over before increasing weight.
Watch, don't add — the consumer-recovery thesis is undercut by 4.2% CPI and elevated energy costs; reassess after the July 14 print.
Hold AVGO — custom AI silicon demand from MSFT/META/Apple is part of the same locked-in capex wave as NVDA; RRSP placement still optimal for the dividend.
Trim BTCC toward the low end of its range — Fear & Greed at 27 plus live Fed and geopolitical uncertainty is not the setup to hold full high-beta weight.
